tasks_completed.changes: Added procedural spec_path patch block after sanity checks in Step 5 (~60 lines). Block uses python3 heredoc with CONTEXT_JSON_PATH + NEW_SPEC_PATH inline env-var passing (codex iter-3 F1 fix). When orchestrator-resolved spec_path is non-null AND current is null/absent/empty/different, overwrite context.json.spec_path. When orchestrator-resolved is null, no patch (key remains absent/null) per codex iter-2 C3. Includes 4-condition matrix table + integration note.
tasks_completed.rationale: Item 1 root cause: BA agent schema has no spec_path field, so context.json never carries it even when --spec passed. Orchestrator-level Step 5 patch is the correct location (after BA validation, before QA dispatch).

tasks_completed.description: Items 3+4 (M3) — hooks/pretool-bash-safety.sh Layer 1.F shlex tokenizer + 4-state quote walker
tasks_completed.changes: Replaced raw-grep compound/write detection with Python helper using shlex.shlex(posix=True, punctuation_chars=True). Helper exhausts list(lex) in try/except ValueError (fail-closed per AC-08/codex C11). Returns BARE_WRITER/PURE_READ/DENY/TOKENIZER_ERROR. Detects: compound separators as standalone tokens after punctuation_chars splitting, recursive shells, eval/source/dot-source, leading VAR=, shell keywords, xargs, extended redirect tokens (>, >>, <, <>, >|, &>, &>>, >&, <&, <<, <<-, <<<), tee, find write-action flags with $-stripped normalization for ANSI-C concatenated forms (codex iter-3 F5). Active subshell/process-sub detection via 4-state quote machine (UNQUOTED/SINGLE/DOUBLE/ANSI_C) that correctly tracks bash semantics: single quotes inside double quotes are LITERAL (not toggle); $( and backticks remain active inside double quotes; <( and >( inert inside double quotes; ANSI-C $'...' handles backslash escapes (codex iter-3 F4). BARE_WRITER and PURE_READ allowlist decisions deferred to AFTER all deny checks (codex iter-3 F3: prior anchored regex matched `script.py ;` because `(\s|$)` accepted whitespace before separator).
tasks_completed.rationale: Items 3+4 root cause: raw-text grep cannot distinguish shell metacharacters inside quoted contexts from actual shell operators. Items 3 (ANSI-C bypass) and 4 (over-blocking) both stem from the same root cause; one fix closes both. Codex iter-3 review surfaced 4 additional regression vectors (F3 compound-after-script, F4 mixed quoting, F5 ANSI-C concatenation, F6 extended redirects); all 4 fixed and verified with 14 new regression cases.

tasks_completed.description: Item 5 (M5) — hooks/pretool-bash-safety.sh entry-gate glob char fix
tasks_completed.changes: Replaced entry-gate regex `[A-Za-z0-9_\-]*\.flag` (which excluded glob metachars) with two `grep -F` substring matches on `/tmp/claude-bulk-allowed-` and `/tmp/claude-bulk-commit-sentinel-`. Substring match is permissive on the path side; actual write/compound detection happens inside the Layer 1.F block via shlex tokenization.
tasks_completed.rationale: Item 5 root cause: character class excluded *, ?, [, ] — so `cat /tmp/claude-bulk-allowed-*.flag` entirely skipped Layer 1.F protection. grep -F substring is the safer alternative to a regex with escaped glob chars (codex iter-1 F5 + iter-2 C10).

tasks_completed.changes: Appended `### Cycle 4`, `### Cycle 5`, `### Cycle 6` subsections to Sections 2, 3, 4, 6, 7, 8 (Section 5 is user's static acceptance criterion — invariant). Each header has non-empty implementation summary content sourced from completion-20260524-172805.md (Cycle 4), completion-20260524-205206.md (Cycle 5), completion-20260525-095242.md (Cycle 6). Section 2 covers approach/implementation; Section 3 covers files modified/created; Section 4 covers QA-measured current state with verdict; Section 6 documents close-debate outcome; Section 7 captures forward-looking remediation plans (including the Cycle 6 → Cycle 7 plan that this task realizes); Section 8 captures attention notes for the codex out-of-scope observations from Cycle 6 (addressed by this Cycle 7 work).
tasks_completed.rationale: Item 6 root cause: manual data entry not yet performed — the spec is a living document but the Cycle 4/5/6 content was orphaned in the per-cycle completion reports.

AC-01 added test_AC_01_step5_spec_path_patch_block_present_in_dev_md asserting 4 required markers in commands/dev.md ('spec_path patch', '20260526-053746', 'orchestrator_resolved_spec_path', 'absent / null / empty / different'). AC-02 is uniquely a runtime check awaiting QA dispatch — skip behavior at dev-time is appropriate, with documented QA-rerun requirement.

git_rationale.root_cause_commit: 867042b — fix(security): close Layer 1.F compound-command bypass in pretool-bash-safety.sh (task 20260525-095242). Introduced the raw-grep compound detector that over-blocks quoted metachars and does not handle ANSI-C reassembly.
git_rationale.why_issue_occurred: Cycle 6 (867042b) added compound-shape detection using grep patterns against raw command text. Valid defense against compound bypasses but did not account for quoting semantics; same-class attacks (ANSI-C $'...' splitting, in-quote |) bypass it.
git_rationale.how_fix_addresses_root: Replaced raw-text grep with Python shlex.shlex(posix=True, punctuation_chars=True) tokenization. The tokenizer correctly: (a) splits unspaced separators into standalone tokens; (b) strips quotes uniformly so quoted metachars are not classified as operators; (c) reassembles ANSI-C $'...' fragments into single tokens with embedded $. The active-subshell/process-sub axis that shlex cannot distinguish is handled by a 4-state quote-state raw-text walker that mirrors bash semantics. Eliminates the entire class of quoting-aware bypass and false-positive bugs.

diff_stats.justification_for_overage: Total > 350-line floor because: (i) Items 3+4 mandated a Python helper inline in bash (the canonical tokenizer recipe + 4-state quote walker is irreducibly ~150 lines); (ii) Item 6 requires real Cycle 4/5/6 content for 6 sections (196 lines of summary content sourced from 3 completion reports); (iii) commands/dev.md Step 5 patch block is procedural-instruction prose for the orchestrator (~60 lines); (iv) codex iter-3 review surfaced 5 additional BLOCKERs (F1/F3/F4/F5/F6) that required hardening — the quote-state walker grew from 30 lines to 90 lines to handle in_double + ANSI-C semantics correctly. The line budget was authorized implicitly by the 6-item dispatch scope; this is one of the largest legitimate cycles in this session series.

codex_consult.feedback_summary: Codex returned 6 in-scope findings (5 BLOCKER + 1 MAJOR) + 2 OBSERVATION_ONLY. BLOCKERs: F1 spec_path patch heredoc missing env-var inline passing (KeyError at runtime); F3 bare-writer regex returned BARE_WRITER before tokenization, allowing `script.py ; touch <flag>` compound-after-script bypass; F4 quote-state walker tracked only in_single, missing in_double — single quotes inside double quotes are LITERAL not toggle, so `"'$(cmd)'"` would bypass; F5 ANSI-C concatenated forms like `-de$'lete'` tokenize to `-de$lete` and bypass the {-delete,$-delete} exact-match set; F6 REDIRECT_TOKENS missing `&>`, `&>>`, `>&`, `>|`, `<<`, `<<-`, `<<<`. MAJOR: same as F6 framed as missing exact tokens. OBSERVATION_ONLY: (1) IS_SUBAGENT correctly flows through Layer 1.F with no exemption; (2) AC-06 test validates first occurrence of headers globally rather than per-section.
codex_consult.feedback_incorporated: All 5 BLOCKERs and the MAJOR fixed: F1 — added CONTEXT_JSON_PATH and NEW_SPEC_PATH inline env-var passing to the python3 heredoc in commands/dev.md. F3 — restructured Python helper so tokenization and ALL deny checks run BEFORE the bare-writer regex; bare-writer is now an allowlist branch that only fires if no compound/write/dangerous shape exists. F4 — replaced the 2-state walker with a 4-state quote machine (UNQUOTED/SINGLE/DOUBLE/ANSI_C) implementing bash semantics correctly: single quotes inside double quotes are literal; $( and backticks remain active inside double quotes; <( and >( are inert inside double quotes; ANSI-C $'...' handles backslash escapes. F5 — added `$`-stripped normalization to the write-action loop: `if '$' in t: normalized = t.replace('$', ''); if normalized in WRITE_ACTION_FLAGS: DENY`. This catches `-de$lete`, `$-de$lete`, `-f$print`, etc. F6 — extended REDIRECT_TOKENS to {>, >>, <, <>, >|, &>, &>>, >&, <&, <<, <<-, <<<}. Re-verified with 14 new regression test cases in /var/tmp/smoke_hook_v2.py: 40/40 PASS (26 original AC + 14 codex regression). AC-02 F2 partial closure — kept skip semantics (intentional dev-time behavior since QA hasn't run yet) but improved the skip message with explicit 'QA must re-run after report exists and treat remaining skip as FAIL' directive. F2-full (enforcing skip→fail in QA Phase 5) requires QA-side logic that is out of scope for this dev task. OBSERVATION_ONLY findings logged but not actioned: F2-full and AC-06 per-section strengthening listed in recommendations[].
